sets.passage:
The migration of office work into the home, once dismissed as a fringe arrangement, became an organizing principle of professional life almost overnight. (1)______ What had been an emergency improvisation hardened into an expectation that many workers now refuse to surrender.

The appeal is easy to understand. Commuting, that daily tax on time and temper, vanished, and the hours it consumed were reabsorbed into family, rest, or productivity. (2)______ Employers, meanwhile, discovered that they could shrink their expensive real-estate footprints without measurable losses in output.

Yet the picture is more complicated than the enthusiasts admit. (3)______ The casual corridor conversation, the overheard problem solved by a passing colleague, the tacit knowledge transferred through proximity, all of these proved difficult to replicate through scheduled video calls. Junior employees, in particular, lost the apprenticeship that observation once provided.

There is also the matter of boundaries, which the early enthusiasm tended to overlook entirely. When the living room doubles as the conference room, the working day tends to expand rather than contract, and the psychological signal that once separated labour from leisure quietly dissolves. (4)______ Burnout, paradoxically, has risen sharply among precisely those workers who were granted the greatest measure of flexibility.

The likely outcome is neither a wholesale return to the crowded cubicle nor its permanent and complete abandonment. (5)______ Such carefully negotiated hybrid models attempt to preserve the autonomy that workers now prize while restoring the incidental human contact upon which organizations have always quietly depended. Whether they succeed will determine not only where we work, but how the very texture of collaborative life is rebuilt for a generation that no longer takes the office for granted.
bank: When the pandemic forced millions to leave their offices, few imagined the change would prove so durable.
Workers reported reclaiming a sense of control over schedules that rigid office hours had long denied them.
Something valuable, however, was quietly lost when the shared physical workplace receded from daily routine.
Many employees now find themselves answering messages late into the evening, unable to switch off entirely.
Most large firms are converging instead on arrangements that mix several office days with remote ones.
The cost of commercial office space has fallen sharply in every major financial centre this decade.
Productivity software companies recorded their highest revenues during the first months of the crisis.
sets.explain_zh: (1)选0：开头讲居家办公突然成为常态，0“疫情迫使数百万人离开办公室，少有人想到改变会如此持久”承接下文“紧急权宜变成持久期望”。(2)选1：前句讲通勤消失、时间被重新分配，1“工人重获对作息的掌控感”与“家庭、休息、产出”的自主性形成词汇链。(3)选2：本段转折“情况比鼓吹者承认的复杂”，2“然而某些有价值的东西悄然失去”用however呼应转折，下文具体列举走廊对话等失去之物。(4)选3：前句讲工作日延长、劳逸界限消失，3“许多员工深夜还在回信息，无法彻底关机”是其具体体现。(5)选4：前句说既不会全回格子间也不会永久废弃，4“多数大公司转向混合办公”正是折中，下文hybrid models呼应。干扰5（写字楼租金下跌）与6（生产力软件收入创新高）均话题相关但：5只是孤立事实，放任何空都断了指代与逻辑链；6讲软件公司营收，与各空的指代/连接词均不匹配，放(2)会与“工人自主”脱节、放(3)无转折功能。

sets.passage:
Every wave of mechanization has been accompanied by a prophecy of mass unemployment, and every wave has so far disappointed the prophets. (1)______ The looms that terrified the weavers eventually demanded mechanics, designers, and managers who had not existed before. The question now is whether intelligent automation behaves like its predecessors or breaks the pattern.

What distinguishes the present moment is the speed and breadth of substitution. (2)______ Earlier machines replaced muscle; today's systems increasingly replace cognition, encroaching on tasks once thought safely human, from drafting contracts to diagnosing disease.

Economists who study these transitions stress that the relevant unit is not the job but the task. (3)______ A radiologist whose image-reading is automated may spend the freed hours counselling patients or coordinating treatment, work that machines handle poorly. The occupation survives even as its constituent activities are reshuffled.

This reframing carries a sharp implication for how societies prepare. (4)______ The premium shifts toward capacities that resist codification: judgement under ambiguity, persuasion, the orchestration of people and machines together. These are precisely the skills our schools, organized around the transmission of facts, are least equipped to cultivate.

The danger, then, is less that work itself will disappear than that the rewards of work will come to divide ever more unequally between groups. (5)______ Whether the coming decades simply repeat that troubling pattern or instead chart a visibly fairer course depends on deliberate choices about training and redistribution that are fundamentally political, not technological. The machines, as always, will do what we design them to do; the harder problem is deciding what we want.
bank: History offers a reassuring record: technology has destroyed particular trades while creating whole new categories of employment.
Most occupations are bundles of activities, only some of which any given machine can take over.
Never before has a single technology threatened to displace so many kinds of work so quickly.
If routine tasks are the first to be automated, then training must emphasize what remains stubbornly non-routine.
Those who already command scarce, hard-to-automate abilities will capture a growing share of the gains.
Governments across the world have begun subsidizing the purchase of industrial robots for small factories.
The earliest mechanical calculators were enormous, unreliable, and affordable only to the largest institutions.
sets.explain_zh: (1)选0：开头讲每次机械化都伴随失业预言却落空，0“历史提供安慰：技术毁掉具体行当却创造全新就业类别”总述，下文织布机例子呼应。(2)选2：本段主题“当下不同在于替代的速度与广度”，2“从未有单一技术威胁如此之快地取代如此多种类的工作”正点出speed and breadth，下文“早期机器替代肌肉、如今替代认知”是其展开。(3)选1：经济学家强调单位是task而非job，1“多数职业是活动的捆绑，机器只能接管其中一些”直接解释“不是job而是task”的视角，下文放射科医生“职业存续而活动重组”的例子呼应。(4)选3：本段讲对社会准备的影响，3“若常规任务先被自动化，则培训须强调仍非常规之物”承接，下文premium shifts呼应。(5)选4：前句讲工作回报将日益不均，4“已掌握稀缺难自动化能力者将攫取更多收益”是不均的机制。干扰5（政府补贴买机器人）与6（早期机械计算器笨重昂贵）：5是孤立政策事实，任何空都缺指代衔接，放(2)讲不了认知替代；6是历史细节，与“技能/不均/task”各空逻辑均不接，放(1)会与“创造新就业”主旨偏离。
sets._topic: automation and the future of skills

sets.passage:
The gig economy promised to dissolve the rigidities of the traditional job and replace them with something lighter, freer, and more responsive to individual choice. (1)______ A driver could log on between school runs; a designer could assemble clients across continents without ever entering an office. For many, this flexibility was not a slogan but a genuine liberation.

The accounting, however, is rarely so simple. Flexibility, on closer inspection, often turns out to be its opposite for those at the bottom of the market. (2)______ When the algorithm assigns the shifts and sets the fares, the worker's celebrated freedom shrinks to the freedom to accept or starve.

The deeper trade-off concerns the safety net. (3)______ Sick pay, pensions, unemployment insurance, and paid holidays were all bundled into the standard contract that gig work deliberately unbundles. A platform classifies its workers as independent contractors, and with a single legal definition the obligations of an employer evaporate.

Defenders counter that millions value the arrangement precisely because it asks nothing of them in return for nothing. (4)______ A retiree topping up a pension, a student earning beer money, a professional smoothing a career gap, each may rationally prefer cash now to protections later. The trouble arises when such work becomes not a supplement but a sole and permanent livelihood.

Regulators are therefore searching for a third category, neither employee nor contractor, that might extend basic protections without abolishing the model. (5)______ The outcome will reveal whether the gig economy can be civilized or whether its freedoms were always built on the quiet transfer of risk from companies to the individuals who serve them.
bank: In its early years the model seemed to deliver exactly what it advertised.
The autonomy the platforms advertise is bounded tightly by the systems that distribute the work.
What the standard job quietly provided, the gig arrangement strips away in the name of efficiency.
For some, after all, the absence of obligation is the whole point of the bargain.
Several countries are now drafting laws to create precisely such an intermediate status.
Ride-hailing applications first appeared in large American cities before spreading worldwide.
Consumers generally report high satisfaction with the speed of on-demand delivery services.
sets.explain_zh: (1)选0：开头讲零工经济承诺自由灵活，0“早年这一模式似乎兑现了广告所言”承接，下文司机、设计师例子是“兑现”的展开。(2)选1：本段转折“灵活常变成反面”，1“平台宣传的自主被分配工作的系统紧紧框住”解释为何灵活变反面，下文“算法派单定价”呼应。(3)选2：本段主题safety net，2“标准工作悄然提供的，零工以效率之名剥去”引出下文病假、养老金等被剥离清单。(4)选3：前句讲拥护者说很多人正因其无义务而珍视，3“毕竟对某些人而言，无义务正是这桩交易的全部意义”用after all承接，下文退休者、学生例子呼应。(5)选4：前句讲监管者寻找第三类身份，4“若干国家正起草法律创设这样的中间身份”直接呼应such an intermediate status。干扰5（打车软件先在美国大城市出现）与6（消费者对配送速度满意）：5是起源事实，无指代衔接，放(1)讲不出“兑现承诺”；6讲消费者满意，与各空（讲工人/保障/监管）逻辑相反或无关，放(4)会与“工人珍视无义务”错位。

sets.passage:
Why does one invention sweep through a society in a decade while another, no less ingenious, languishes for a generation? The diffusion of innovation, it turns out, obeys a social logic at least as powerful as the technical merits of the thing itself. (1)______ Adoption is a decision made by people embedded in networks, and people watch one another far more than they study specifications.

Researchers have long observed that new practices spread in a characteristic curve. (2)______ A small, adventurous minority experiments first, absorbing the risk and the ridicule. Only when these pioneers are seen to prosper does a cautious majority begin to follow, and the curve steepens into something resembling an epidemic.

Crucial to this whole process is the underrated role of the trusted intermediary. (3)______ A cautious farmer is far more likely to plant an unfamiliar seed because a respected neighbour has already done so than because some distant expert merely recommended it in a pamphlet. Proximity and credibility, not the bare supply of information, carry the decisive weight here.

The attributes of the innovation matter too, though not always the ones engineers emphasize. (4)______ A technology that can be tried cheaply, observed easily, and reversed without ruin spreads faster than one demanding an irreversible leap of faith, however superior the latter may be on paper.

Understanding these dynamics has practical force. (5)______ The lesson is consistent: to change what people do, one must first understand whom they trust and how they learn from each other, for the social fabric is the true medium through which the new becomes ordinary.
bank: A superior product, the evidence repeatedly shows, does not automatically guarantee its own success.
This pattern, plotted over time, traces the familiar S-shaped path from a trickle to a flood.
Ideas tend to travel along the lines of personal relationship rather than across them.
Ease of trial and visibility of results often predict uptake better than raw performance does.
Public-health campaigns that recruit local opinion leaders consistently outperform those that simply broadcast facts.
The printing press was invented in Europe during the middle of the fifteenth century.
Consumers in wealthy nations now own more electronic devices than ever before in history.
sets.explain_zh: (1)选0：开头讲扩散遵循社会逻辑，胜过技术本身，0“优越产品并不自动保证成功”是这一论点的直接表述，下文“人们彼此观察甚于研究参数”呼应。(2)选1：本段讲新实践沿特征曲线传播，1“此模式随时间描出从涓滴到洪流的S形路径”定义曲线，下文“少数先锋—多数跟随—曲线陡升”是S形展开。(3)选2：本段主题“可信中介”，2“观念沿人际关系线传播而非跨越之”阐释中介，下文“农民因邻居而非远方专家而种新种”呼应。(4)选3：本段讲创新属性，3“可试性与结果可见性常比纯性能更能预测采纳”承接，下文“可廉价试、易观察、可逆者传播更快”呼应。(5)选4：前句讲理解动态有实践意义，4“招募本地意见领袖的公卫运动持续优于单纯广播事实者”是实践例证。干扰5（印刷术15世纪中叶发明于欧洲）与6（富国消费者拥有更多电子设备）：5是孤立历史事实，无S曲线/信任/属性等衔接，放(2)讲不出曲线；6是消费统计，与“扩散逻辑/信任/属性”各空均不构成指代或因果，放(5)无法承接“实践意义”。

sets.passage:
The recent triumphs of artificial intelligence have been so vivid that it is tempting to assume the remaining obstacles are merely matters of scale. Feed the machines more data, the reasoning goes, and general intelligence will eventually emerge. (1)______ The systems that astonish us excel at interpolation within the vast territory of their training, yet stumble the moment they are asked to step beyond it.

Consider the strange brittleness that so often lurks beneath the surface of fluent performance. (2)______ A model that answers a thousand difficult questions flawlessly may suddenly collapse on the thousand-and-first if it happens to be phrased in a way that no training example ever anticipated, betraying that it has merely matched surface patterns rather than genuinely grasped any meaning.

A second limit concerns the absence of a model of the world. (3)______ The system manipulates symbols whose referents it has never touched, producing sentences about gravity or grief with no experience of falling or loss. Coherence is not comprehension, and eloquence can mask a hollow core.

Then there is the stubborn and ancient problem of causation. (4)______ Correlations harvested passively from data can tell the machine what tends to accompany what, but never what would actually happen if the world itself were deliberately and forcibly changed. Without that grasp, its predictions falter precisely when circumstances depart from the familiar.

None of this is to dismiss the technology, whose practical value is already immense. (5)______ Recognizing where the present paradigm reaches its edge is not pessimism but realism, and it is the necessary first step toward whatever genuinely intelligent machines might one day require.
bank: A growing body of evidence, however, suggests that scale alone will not carry us all the way there.
These systems lack the robustness that even a young child brings to an unfamiliar situation.
Human understanding is grounded in bodies that move through and act upon a physical reality.
Statistical association, however abundant, is not the same as knowing why one event produces another.
It is rather to insist that admiration be tempered by a clear sense of the limits.
The computing power devoted to training such models has doubled roughly every few months.
Many companies have rushed to embed chatbots into their customer-service operations.
sets.explain_zh: (1)选0：开头讲人们以为只差规模，0“然而越来越多证据表明仅靠规模无法走完全程”用however转折，下文“擅长内插却越界即失败”呼应。(2)选1：本段主题“脆性”，1“这些系统缺乏连幼童面对陌生情境都具备的稳健性”定义脆性，下文“换种没见过的问法就崩溃”呼应。(3)选2：本段讲缺乏世界模型，2“人类理解植根于在物理现实中行动的身体”正是“世界模型”的内涵，下文“操纵从未触及指称物的符号”呼应。(4)选3：本段主题causation，3“统计关联再丰富也不等于知道何以一事致另一事”直接点出因果与相关之别，下文呼应。(5)选4：前句讲不否定其巨大实用价值，4“而是坚持赞赏须以清醒认识其局限来调和”承接but转折语气，下文“认识边界是现实主义”呼应。干扰5（算力每几月翻倍）与6（公司争相嵌入客服聊天机器人）：5支持“规模会成功”，恰与各空“规模不够/有局限”主旨相反，放(1)直接矛盾；6是商业应用事实，与脆性/世界模型/因果各空无指代衔接，放(2)讲不出robustness。

sets.passage:
The feeds that now mediate so much of our cultural and political life are not neutral windows onto the world. They are curated by recommendation algorithms whose single overriding objective is to hold attention, and that objective quietly reshapes what billions of people see, believe, and feel. (1)______ The consequences ripple outward in ways their designers neither intended nor fully control.

Consider first the consequential matter of what gets amplified and what gets buried. (2)______ Content that reliably provokes outrage, fear, or fierce tribal loyalty consistently outperforms the measured and the moderate post, simply because strong emotion is what keeps a restless thumb scrolling down the page. The algorithm does not prefer falsehood, but it rewards intensity, and intensity and accuracy are not the same thing.

A second effect concerns the narrowing of the shared world. (3)______ Each user is fed an ever more precisely tailored stream, and two neighbours may inhabit informational universes that scarcely overlap. The common ground on which democratic argument depends erodes one personalized recommendation at a time.

The defenders of these systems make a reasonable point. (4)______ No engineer sat down intending to inflame societies; the harms emerged from optimizing a reasonable-sounding metric without anticipating its social weight. Intent, however, offers cold comfort to those living with the results.

The encouraging news, at least in principle, is that the very same levers that produced these effects can also be adjusted. (5)______ Whether the powerful companies that own and tune the algorithms will ever voluntarily accept such constraints, when sustained attention remains the principal source of their revenue, is the unresolved question on which a very great deal now depends.
bank: What began as a tool for sorting abundance has become an architect of perception.
The systems learn, through relentless experiment, which posts trigger the strongest reaction.
Personalization, pursued far enough, fragments the audience into countless isolated enclaves.
The algorithms were never programmed to polarize; they were programmed to maximize engagement.
Designers could optimize for accuracy or diversity rather than for raw time spent on the platform.
Social-media platforms generated hundreds of billions of dollars in advertising revenue last year.
The first photo-sharing applications became popular among smartphone users about a decade ago.
sets.explain_zh: (1)选0：开头讲算法以留住注意力为目标，悄然重塑认知，0“起初是整理信息洪流的工具，如今成了感知的建筑师”总括，下文“后果向外涟漪”呼应。(2)选1：本段主题“何物被放大”，1“系统通过不断试验学会哪些帖子触发最强反应”解释放大机制，下文“激怒恐惧帖更胜出”呼应。(3)选2：本段讲共享世界变窄，2“个性化推到极致把受众碎裂成无数孤岛”定义narrowing，下文“两邻居信息宇宙几无交集”呼应。(4)选3：本段讲辩护者的合理之处，3“算法从未被设计来制造对立，而是被设计来最大化参与度”正是其合理论点，下文“无工程师意在煽动”呼应。(5)选4：前句讲同样的杠杆可被调整，4“设计者本可为准确或多样而非纯停留时长去优化”是“调整”的具体内容。干扰5（社媒去年广告收入数千亿）与6（首批照片分享应用约十年前流行）：5是营收事实，虽与结尾“注意力是收入来源”话题沾边，但作整句填入各空都缺指代衔接，放(5)位会与“可调整杠杆”逻辑断裂；6是起源细节，与放大/碎片化/意图各空无任何衔接，放(2)讲不出“学会触发反应”。
sets._topic: the social impact of recommendation algorithms

sets.passage:
For every technology that reshapes the world, a graveyard holds dozens that promised as much and delivered nothing. The reasons for failure are seldom the ones outsiders assume, who suppose that being the best product is what guarantees a winner. (1)______ A device may be cheaper, faster, and demonstrably superior and still die in the marketplace, because adoption depends on forces that have little to do with the laboratory.

The first of these is the burden of the existing system. (2)______ A new keyboard layout that types more efficiently must overcome the millions of fingers already trained on the old one, the factories tooled to make it, the habits woven into every workplace. Inertia, accumulated over decades, is a formidable opponent.

A second obstacle is the absence of a supporting ecosystem. (3)______ An electric car without charging stations, a video format without players, a brilliant application without users, each is a key cut for a lock that does not yet exist. The pieces must arrive together, and coordinating that arrival is fiendishly hard.

Then there is the question of who bears the cost and who reaps the benefit. (4)______ When the people asked to change are not the people who gain, even a socially valuable innovation can stall, blocked by a misalignment that no amount of engineering can fix.

The pattern that emerges is humbling for inventors. (5)______ The other ingredients in that recipe, so easy to overlook from inside the laboratory, are precisely the tangled social, economic, and infrastructural ones, and reading them is an art the workshop rarely teaches.
bank: Technical excellence, the historical record insists, is neither necessary nor sufficient for success.
Any innovation must compete against an incumbent already deeply entrenched in daily life.
Some technologies are useless until a whole network of complementary products surrounds them.
Innovations frequently fail because their costs and rewards fall on entirely different parties.
Merit, it turns out, is only one ingredient in a recipe with many less obvious components.
The number of patents filed worldwide has increased steadily over the past several decades.
Engineers typically receive far less public recognition than the entrepreneurs who commercialize their work.
sets.explain_zh: (1)选0：前句明说外人“以为做出最好的产品就保证成功”，0“技术卓越对成功既非必要也非充分”正面否定这一“最好即保证”的假设，并与下文“更便宜更快更优仍夭折”形成必要充分性的词汇链；干扰项4虽也讲卓越，但不直接否定‘保证’命题，且其‘配方/成分’比喻在(1)处无任何下文锚点，故不入(1)。(2)选1：本段主题“既有系统的负担”，1“任何创新须与已深植日常的在位者竞争”定义burden，下文“新键盘须战胜训练好的手指/工厂/习惯”呼应。(3)选2：本段讲缺乏支撑生态，2“有些技术在互补产品网络环绕之前毫无用处”阐释ecosystem，下文“无充电桩的电车/无播放器的格式”呼应。(4)选3：本段讲成本与收益归属错位，3“创新常因成本与回报落于完全不同方而失败”直接点题，下文“被要求改变者非受益者”呼应。(5)选4：前句讲这一规律令发明者谦卑，4“原来卓越只是配方中诸多不显眼成分之一”承接；其后句明言“配方中其余的成分（the other ingredients in that recipe）恰是错综的社会、经济、基础设施因素”，唯有含‘配方/成分’意象的4能充当该句的先行设定，0的‘必要充分’措辞在此无锚点，故0、4在(1)(5)间不可互换。干扰5（全球专利数十年稳增）与6（工程师所获公众认可少于创业者）：5是专利统计，与失败原因各空无指代/因果衔接，放(1)讲不出“非必要非充分”；6讲认可归属，看似与“成本/收益”沾边，但讲的是声誉而非创新失败机制，作整句放(4)会偏离“成本回报错位致失败”，且无下文衔接。
sets._topic: why some technologies fail to spread

sets.passage:
As work moves online, the tools that record it have grown quietly omnivorous. Keystrokes, mouse movements, the contents of screens, the duration of every pause, all can now be captured, scored, and stored by software that watches employees as closely as any foreman ever did. (1)______ The technology has outrun the ethical frameworks meant to govern it, and the gap is widening.

Managers defend the practice in the language of fairness and efficiency. (2)______ If output can be measured precisely, they argue, then reward can be allocated justly and slackers cannot hide behind their diligent colleagues. There is a genuine logic here, and dismissing it as mere control would be too easy.

Yet the costs are real and frequently underestimated. (3)______ Workers who know they are continuously assessed tend to optimize for the metric rather than the mission, gaming the measure while the true purpose of their labour quietly suffers. Trust, once the invisible lubricant of organizational life, corrodes under the constant gaze.

There is also a question of proportion that the technology tempts us to ignore. (4)______ A measure justified for a safety-critical role may be wildly excessive for a salaried professional, yet the same software is sold and deployed indiscriminately across both. Capability, in other words, has been mistaken for warrant.

The path forward is not to ban the tools but to govern them. (5)______ Surveillance that is transparent, proportionate, and accountable to those it observes may be tolerable; surveillance that is none of these things corrodes the very productivity it claims to serve.
bank: What was once impractical at scale has suddenly become cheap and routine.
Monitoring, on this view, simply makes hidden performance visible and therefore manageable.
Constant observation reshapes behaviour in ways that often defeat its own purpose.
Just because something can be tracked does not mean that tracking it is warranted.
Clear limits on what is collected, and for what stated purpose, are the obvious starting point.
Office buildings have installed security cameras in their lobbies for many years.
Remote workers tend to log on earlier in the morning than their office-based peers.
sets.explain_zh: (1)选0：开头讲监控工具变得无所不录，0“过去大规模上不切实际之事，忽然变得廉价而寻常”解释为何技术井喷，下文“技术跑赢了伦理框架”呼应。(2)选1：本段讲管理者以公平效率辩护，1“依此看监控只是把隐藏的绩效变得可见从而可管”是其辩护内容，下文“可精确度量则可公正奖惩”呼应。(3)选2：本段转折讲代价，2“持续观察以常挫败自身目的的方式重塑行为”点题，下文“工人迎合指标而非使命”呼应。(4)选3：本段主题“比例”，3“能被追踪不等于追踪它有正当性”直接点出warrant问题，下文“安全岗合理之度对白领则过度”呼应。(5)选4：前句讲出路是治理而非禁用，4“对收集何物、为何目的设清晰界限是显然的起点”是治理的具体起点。干扰5（写字楼大堂多年装监控摄像头）与6（远程工作者比坐班同事更早上线）：5是物理安防事实，与“数字监控伦理”各空无指代衔接，放(1)讲不出“忽然廉价寻常”的数字监控；6是行为统计，与辩护/代价/比例各空无逻辑关系，放(3)无法表达“观察挫败目的”。

sets.passage:
There was a time when an expert was simply someone who knew more than the rest of us, and we deferred accordingly. That comfortable arrangement has frayed. (1)______ The internet placed a library in every pocket, and with it the illusion, sometimes the reality, that anyone could acquire in an afternoon what once took years of study.

This democratization of information has genuine virtues. (2)______ A patient who arrives informed can question a diagnosis; a citizen who reads the data can scrutinize a policy. Authority that cannot survive examination perhaps did not deserve to survive in the first place.

Yet the same flood that empowers also disorients. (3)______ Distinguishing the careful study from the confident fabrication requires precisely the trained judgement that expertise was supposed to provide, and which the amateur, by definition, lacks. Access to information is not the same as the capacity to evaluate it.

Meanwhile, the experts themselves have contributed to their own decline. (4)______ When specialists overreach, predicting with false precision or speaking outside their competence, each public failure is remembered and generalized, eroding trust in the very institutions that knowledge depends upon. Humility, paradoxically, would have protected their authority.

What seems to be emerging from all this turbulence is a more demanding compact between experts and the public. (5)______ Expertise must now earn its standing continuously, through honest transparency about its own uncertainty and a genuine willingness to show its reasoning openly, rather than claiming deference by credential and title alone. The expert of the future will be less an oracle to be obeyed than a guide whose work invites, and survives, our scrutiny.
bank: The very meaning of expertise is being renegotiated before our eyes.
When knowledge is widely shared, those who hold it can no longer simply command obedience.
Abundance of information brings with it an abundance of misinformation indistinguishable to the untrained eye.
Their credibility has been wounded as much by their own overconfidence as by public scepticism.
Deference is no longer granted automatically; it has to be continually re-earned.
Universities awarded a record number of advanced degrees over the past decade.
Search engines process billions of individual queries on a typical day.
sets.explain_zh: (1)选0：开头讲专家曾理所当然受敬，如今瓦解，0“专长的含义正在我们眼前被重新协商”总括，下文“互联网把图书馆装进口袋”呼应。(2)选1：本段讲信息民主化的优点，1“知识广泛共享时，持有者不能再单凭命令要人服从”引出下文“知情病人可质疑诊断”。(3)选2：本段转折“同样的洪流也使人迷失”，2“信息丰沛伴随同样丰沛、外行难辨的错误信息”点题，下文“分辨严谨研究与自信编造需受训判断”呼应。(4)选3：本段讲专家自毁，3“其公信力既受公众怀疑之伤，也受自身过度自信之伤”点题，下文“专家越界则失败被记住放大”呼应。(5)选4：本段讲新契约，前句导入，4“敬重不再自动给予，须持续重新赢得”是新契约的核心，下文“专长须持续赢得地位”呼应。干扰5（大学十年授予创纪录高学位）与6（搜索引擎日处理数十亿查询）：5是学位统计，与“专长含义/民主化/自毁/新契约”各空无指代衔接，放(1)讲不出“含义被重新协商”；6是搜索量事实，虽与“信息”沾边，但作整句放(3)无法表达“错误信息难辨”这一评价性论断，且无下文衔接。

sets.passage:
The classroom has proven remarkably resistant to change. For a century, while almost every other institution was transformed by technology, the basic scene of education, a teacher addressing rows of students, endured largely intact. (1)______ The question is whether the digital tools now flooding into schools will quietly fade like the rest or instead mark a genuine turning point.

The optimistic case is substantial. (2)______ A lecture can be paused and replayed until it is understood; a struggling student can receive practice tailored to the exact gap in their knowledge; a learner in a remote village can reach instruction once reserved for the privileged few. Distance and pace, the old tyrannies of schooling, begin to loosen their grip.

The sceptics, however, point to a stubborn record of disappointment. (3)______ Film, radio, television, and the teaching machine each occupies an entry in that catalogue: each was greeted as the device that would finally revolutionize learning, and each was eventually absorbed without transforming much at all. Technology, they note, is easy to install and hard to integrate.

The deeper insight may be that education is not primarily a transfer of information. (4)______ Motivation, the modelling of curiosity, the subtle social pressure of a shared room, these are the things that move a reluctant mind, and they are precisely what a screen struggles to supply. A child can access every lecture ever recorded and still learn nothing without the will to engage.

The likeliest future is therefore neither the death of the school nor its digital salvation. (5)______ In such a model the machine handles what machines do well, the drill and the delivery, while the teacher is freed for what only humans can do, the encouragement and the judgement on which real learning has always depended.
bank: Generations of new inventions have arrived promising to remake the school, and most have quietly faded.
Digital tools can personalize instruction in ways a single teacher facing thirty pupils never could.
The history of educational technology is littered with revolutions that never arrived.
It is also, and perhaps mainly, a relationship between human beings.
The promising path blends the technological and the human rather than choosing between them.
Schools in many countries have purchased tablets for every enrolled student.
Online courses can enrol many thousands of participants from around the globe at once.
sets.explain_zh: (1)选0：开头讲课堂百年抗拒变化、师生场景大体未变，0“一代代新发明都承诺重塑学校，多数却悄然消退（most have quietly faded）”顺承“抗拒变化”；下句明问数字工具“会否像其余那样悄然消退（will quietly fade like the rest）”，‘fade … the rest’与0的‘quietly faded’同词复现并要求一个先前消退群体作先行，唯0满足，干扰项2用‘never arrived’而非‘fade’，无法承接，故0、2不可互换入(1)。(2)选1：本段首句“乐观的理由很充分”，1“数字工具能以单个老师面对三十名学生所无法企及的方式实现个性化教学”正是乐观理由的总述，下文“讲座可暂停重放、按知识缺口定制练习、偏远学童也能接触优质教学”是其逐项展开。(3)选2：本段首句讲怀疑者“指向一连串令人失望的记录”，2“教育技术史上满是从未到来的革命（The history … is littered with …）”呼应“失望记录”，并引出一个‘历史/目录’名词；下句“电影、广播、电视与教学机各占该目录（that catalogue）中一席”里的‘that catalogue’必须回指一个‘史/目录’先行项，唯2提供，0只讲发明消退、未引入可供回指的目录名词，故0、2不可互换入(3)。(4)选3：本段首句“更深的洞见在于教育主要并非信息传递”，3“它也、或许主要是人与人之间的一种关系”用also承接并补全“并非信息传递”的另一面，下文“动机、好奇心示范、共处一室的社会压力”正是这种人际关系的内涵。(5)选4：前句讲最可能的未来既非学校消亡也非数字救赎，4“有希望的路径是融合技术与人，而非二者择一”正是这一折中，下文“机器做擅长之事、教师腾出手做唯人能为之事”是融合的具体分工。干扰5（许多国家为每个在校生购置平板）与6（在线课程可一次招收全球数千人）均话题相关却处处失配：5是孤立采购事实，放任何空都缺指代衔接，置于(2)无法充当“乐观理由总述”、置于(3)无‘史/目录’义、也给不出下句‘that catalogue’的先行项；6是规模事实，与“个性化/人际关系/融合”各空逻辑不接，置于(4)与“教育是人际关系”相悖、置于(5)给不出“融合而非二选一”之意。
